messages.subject: [R-sig-Geo] GDAL user survey (open til 2024-11-11)
messages.body_plain:
Very many spatial packages in R depend on the GDAL library (see a rough
listing of packages below, if you use any of these or packages they depend
on it's very likely you are a user of GDAL too).

>From gdal.org, and announced on gdal-dev:

https://lists.osgeo.org/pipermail/gdal-dev/2024-October/059730.html

> The GDAL project is currently soliciting feedback to help focus GDAL
Sponsorship Program activities.

> We would highly appreciate you fill in the survey that will provide
guidance about priorities for the program's resources (open until November
11th, 2024).

The survey is available here:

https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdMXygtDb5M0Ov0KK0u2wKkev1rMqAjRdTlwMeCl7Z1TGJTLw/viewform

Please fill it out to help guide future efforts in the GDAL project.

db <- tools::CRAN_package_db()
writeLines(db$Package[(grepl("GDAL", db$SystemRequirements))])
bamm
concaveman
CoordinateCleaner
deforestable
ebvcube
ech
evapoRe
extRatum
gdalcubes
gdalraster
geouy
ggseg
GWnnegPCA
GWpcor
happign
mapme.biodiversity
nlrx
pRecipe
RCzechia
sapo
sf
smile
spatsoc
terra
vapour
VicmapR
warbleR

Cheers, Mike




--
Michael Sumner
Research Software Engineer
Australian Antarctic Division
Hobart, Australia
e-mail: mdsumner at gmail.com

messages.subject: [R-sig-Geo] spmodel variable importance and marginal effects or partial dependence plots
messages.subject_clean: spmodel variable importance and marginal effects or partial dependence plots
messages.body_plain:
Dear list members,

I am working with the package spmodel, specifically with the spglm
function. Is it possible to assess variable importance and obtain marginal
effects or partial dependence plots for the variables?

Manuel

messages.subject: [R-sig-Geo] Master Evidence Synthesis with Meta-analysis in R (Online Course - Feb 10-14, 2025)
messages.subject_clean: Master Evidence Synthesis with Meta-analysis in R (Online Course - Feb 10-14, 2025)
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
 
registrations are now open for the online course on Meta-analysis in R.
 
This online course (Feb 10-14, 2025) equips you with the skills to conduct robust evidence synthesis  using meta-analysis in R.
 
It includes a mix of lectures and hands-on exercises using real meta-analytic datasets. The emphasis throughout the course is on the application of the various methods and the interpretation of the results using the free software R and the R packages ?metafor? (Viechtbauer 2010) and ?orchaRd? (Nakagawa et al. 2023). The course will follow the principles of open science, with a strong focus on the importance of adhering to preferred reporting items for systematic reviews and meta-analyses (PRISMA EcoEvo; O?Dea et al. 2021). Throughout, we will consider examples of how to interpret results and present them using tables and data visualization, and for each step, we will provide literature and practical resources (e.g., R scripts).
 
For more information, please visit: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/metain-r/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/metain-r/ )
 
Best regards,
Carlo
 
 
 
 

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

mobile: +49 17645230846





	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-Geo] Online course: GIS in R (13 -17 January 2025 )
messages.subject_clean: Online course: GIS in R (13 -17 January 2025 )
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We?re excited to announce our upcoming online course: "Mapping and Spatial Operations in R: Using R as a GIS", scheduled from 13-17 January 2025.
 
This course offers a comprehensive introduction to using R as a Geographic Information System (GIS) for automating and reproducing spatial data analysis. It combines theoretical lessons with practical hands-on sessions, providing participants with the skills to map and analyze environmental and biodiversity data efficiently.
Who Should Attend?
This course is ideal for researchers and practitioners eager to script reproducible and automatable spatial analysis workflows in R. A basic familiarity with R is recommended, but all scripts will be provided and explained in detail.
Learning Outcomes
By the end of this course, you will be able to:
Understand GIS basics, mapping, and cartographic projections.
Import spatial data into R and create detailed maps.
Perform diverse spatial operations and export results.
 
Seats are limited! Visit our website for more details and registration: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/gis-in-r/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/gis-in-r/ )
 
Best regards,
Carlo


--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org
